{"id":"project-LICHEN-vcjj","title":"_find_sender checks peer before peer_lookup_all, may miss correct peer","description":"File: python/src/lichen/link/link_layer.py:562-574

After re-analysis, this is NOT a bug but subtle code worth documenting.

The condition '(peer is None or candidate.pubkey != peer.pubkey)' is an optimization to avoid re-verifying a pubkey that was already tried. If peer_lookup(b'') returned a peer and verify() failed, we skip that peer in brute-force since we already know it doesn't verify.

Downgrading to P4 (documentation). The logic is correct but the interaction between peer_lookup and peer_lookup_all callbacks is subtle and should be documented.","status":"open","priority":4,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"me@mark.atwood.name","created_at":"2026-07-12T06:22:47.329386Z","created_by":"Mark Atwood","updated_at":"2026-07-12T06:23:03.770487Z"}

{"id":"project-LICHEN-vcjj","title":"_find_sender checks peer before peer_lookup_all, may miss correct peer","description":"File: python/src/lichen/link/link_layer.py:562-574

After re-analysis, this is NOT a bug but subtle code worth documenting.

The condition '(peer is None or candidate.pubkey != peer.pubkey)' is an optimization to avoid re-verifying a pubkey that was already tried. If peer_lookup(b'') returned a peer and verify() failed, we skip that peer in brute-force since we already know it doesn't verify.

Downgrading to P4 (documentation). The logic is correct but the interaction between peer_lookup and peer_lookup_all callbacks is subtle and should be documented.","status":"in_progress","priority":4,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"AI Agent","owner":"me@mark.atwood.name","created_at":"2026-07-12T06:22:47.329386Z","created_by":"Mark Atwood","updated_at":"2026-07-23T04:29:39.468662683Z","started_at":"2026-07-23T04:29:39.468662683Z","lease_expires_at":"2026-07-23T04:34:39.468662683Z","heartbeat_at":"2026-07-23T04:29:39.468662683Z"}
